prose: Fukui reactivity descriptors were computed for acrolein using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP in the gas phase (charge 0, multiplicity 1, neutral parent; cation and anion states at multiplicities 2 each). The calculation used PySCF with Mulliken charge partitioning on the user-supplied acrolein.xyz geometry (single-point, not optimized at DFT). The cation and anion states both converged successfully, with charge-conservation checks confirming f⁺ and f⁻ summing correctly to 1.0 each. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy. The most electrophilic site is C (atom index 3, terminal vinyl carbon) with Fukui f⁺ = 0.6423 and dual descriptor = 0.5549, indicating strong preference for nucleophilic addition. The most nucleophilic site is O (atom index 0, carbonyl oxygen) with Fukui f⁻ = 0.3939. Output files written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp8zgkt_w0/live.json and a PNG bar chart to /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260704-174102_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
